activities_projects.title: Comparing Development Goals
activities_projects.objective: To understand that different people have different development goals.
steps: List development goals of a farmer, student, and businessperson.
Compare the goals and find similarities and differences.
Write a short conclusion.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that development goals vary based on individual needs.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check relevance of goals listed and clarity of comparison.

activities_projects.title: Income vs Quality of Life Chart
activities_projects.objective: To analyze income and non-income factors of development.
steps: Draw two columns: Income Factors and Non-Income Factors.
Fill each column with examples from the textbook.
Explain which factors are more important and why.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that development includes both income and quality of life aspects.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess accuracy of examples and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Per Capita Income Calculation
activities_projects.objective: To calculate and interpret per capita income.
steps: Given total income and population of two countries, calculate per capita income.
Compare the results.
State one limitation of per capita income.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn calculation and limitations of per capita income.

activities_projects.title: Human Development Index Table
steps: List the three components of HDI.
Collect HDI data of India and one developed country.
Compare and write two observations.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how HDI measures development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate correctness of data and observations.

activities_projects.title: Local Public Facilities Study
activities_projects.objective: To examine availability of public facilities in the area.
steps: Survey five households about water, schools, hospitals.
Record responses in a table.
Suggest two improvements.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of public facilities in development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check completeness of survey and suggestions.

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Development Poster with Explanation
activities_projects.objective: To connect development with environmental protection.
steps: Draw a poster on sustainable development.
Add two practical examples (e.g., rainwater harvesting).
Explain the importance in 4-5 lines.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the need for sustainable development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess relevance of examples and clarity of explanation.

activities_projects.title: State Comparison Graph
activities_projects.objective: To compare development indicators between two states.
steps: Collect literacy rate and per capita income of two states.
Draw a simple bar graph.
Write two conclusions from the graph.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check accuracy of graph and conclusions.

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Gender and Development
activities_projects.objective: To analyze the role of gender equality in development.
steps: Identify one example of gender inequality.
Explain how it affects development.
Suggest two solutions.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the link between gender equality and development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate depth of explanation and practicality of solutions.

activities_projects.title: Economic Growth vs Development Analysis
activities_projects.objective: To differentiate between economic growth and overall development.
steps: Define economic growth and development.
List two differences between them.
Give one real-life example.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the difference between growth and development.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check correctness of definitions and example.

activities_projects.title: Future Development Plan for Community
activities_projects.objective: To apply development concepts to real-life planning.
steps: Identify three development problems in your community.
Suggest practical solutions.
Present the plan in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply development ideas to real-world situations.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess feasibility of solutions and clarity of presentation.
